weather_report:
Subiaco wakes to a clear, generous sky, the light already bright and clean, sharpening edges on verandas and jacaranda bark. Late morning leans sunny and uncomplicated; by early afternoon a few stray clouds may wander through, softening the glare without stealing the day. Expect strong sunshine for much of the midday window, with UV that can still bite in autumn—shade, sunscreen, and good sunglasses will serve you well. The air reads as clear and dry, a day for long views and simple plans.

It looks like a pleasant, gently warm stretch best met in light layers, breathable fabrics, and a brimmed hat; carry water and you’re set. Consider a slow walk to the shops, a coffee under an awning where you can watch the light move along the footpath, a quick visit to the library by bike, or tending balcony herbs while the sun is kind. Windows open at home will freshen rooms, and laundry will dry gladly on the line. Later, when a few clouds arrive, the light will mellow enough for a comfortable pause on a park bench with a book.
